key_insight: Interpretable vision-transformers can classify fracture causes in zirconia-toughened alumina effectively at low magnification.
engineering_relevance: This approach enhances quality assurance in hip and knee implants, potentially improving patient safety and reducing inspection time.
method: Utilizes a curated dataset of 8,493 SEM images.
Employs a fine-tuned vision-transformer (ViT) model for classification.
Achieves high accuracy and F1 scores under class imbalance.
evidence_signal: The fine-tuned ViT model reached an accuracy of 0.907 and a macro-F1 score of 0.888 in cross-validation.
watch_item: There may be limitations regarding the model's generalizability to other materials or manufacturing processes.
paper_title: Low-Magnification SEM May Suffice: Interpretable Deep Learning for Multi-Scale Fracture-Cause Classification in Zirconia-Toughened Alumina
